# idle

```go
import "keyop/x/idle"
```

Package idle monitors macOS user idle/active state and provides utilities for recording activity, persisting per\-day
summaries, and generating nightly idle reports.

Overview The idle package uses macOS APIs to determine whether the local user is currently "active" or "idle" \(based on
a configurable threshold\). On each periodic Check\(\) the service emits a "status\_update" message and associated
metrics; these messages are used to derive per\-host active periods and to generate daily reports.

Configuration The service can be configured via the ServiceConfig.Config map. Supported configuration keys \(all
optional unless noted\):

- threshold: string \(duration\) The duration after which the user is considered idle, e.g. "5m". If omitted or
  unparsable the default is 5m.

- idle\_metric\_name: string The metric name used when emitting idle duration metrics. If omitted a default of
  \<service\>.idle\_duration is used.

- active\_metric\_name: string The metric name used when emitting active duration metrics. If omitted a default of
  \<service\>.active\_duration is used.

- report\_queue\_file: string Optional template path to a queue file containing serialized messages for a given day. The
  template may include the token "yyyymmdd" which will be replaced with a date in 20060102 format \(for example: "
  \~/logs/keyop/queue.yyyymmdd.log"\). If queue\_file is not configured nightly reports are skipped.

Queue file format \(events\) For nightly reporting the package can read a queue file that contains one JSON message per
line. Each line may be either a legacy core.Message JSON object or a core.Envelope \(the code will attempt to unmarshal
an Envelope first and fall back to a Message\). Lines that cannot be parsed are ignored. Only messages with Event == "
status\_update" and timestamps that fall within the target day are considered when constructing reports.

Example queue line \(envelope style\):

```
{"version":"1","id":"...","payload":{...}}
```

Example legacy message line \(simplified\):

```
{"event":"status_update","hostname":"myhost","timestamp":"2023-10-31T09:01:12-07:00","status":"active"}
```

Report generation Nightly reports are generated by maybeSendIdleReport and are intended to run once per day \(the
service attempts generation between 00:00 and 01:00 local time for the previous calendar day\). When generating a report
the service will:

- determine the queue file path by replacing the token "yyyymmdd" in the configured queue\_file template with the
  previous day's date
- read the file and parse one JSON message per line
- collect all "status\_update" messages for each host that fall within the previous day
- construct per\-host active periods by tracking transitions between status values and aggregating contiguous active
  spans
- merge active periods across hosts to compute total active time and create coverage intervals to calculate known vs
  unknown seconds
- compute totals \(active, idle, unknown\) and format a Markdown report that is emitted as a core.Message with
  Event == "idle\_report"

Notes

- If report\_queue\_file is not configured nightly reports are skipped.
- Local YAML day\-file support has been removed; report generation uses the events queue file when configured.

Usage Initialize the service via NewService\(deps, cfg\) and provide the desired config keys described above. Register
payloads with a core.PayloadRegistry using RegisterPayloads. The service's Check\(\) method should be invoked on a
periodic schedule by the host runtime \(the package expects to run only on macOS/darwin\).

Package idle report logic for generating daily idle reports from the queue file.

Package idle monitors macOS user idle/active state and emits events used for presence and automation.

<a name="ActivePeriod"></a>
## type [ActivePeriod](<https://github.com/wu/keyop/blob/main/x/idle/service.go#L25-L30>)

ActivePeriod represents a single active period within a day file.

```go
type ActivePeriod struct {
    Hostname        string    `yaml:"hostname" json:"hostname"`
    Start           time.Time `yaml:"start" json:"start"`
    Stop            time.Time `yaml:"stop" json:"stop"`
    DurationSeconds float64   `yaml:"durationSeconds" json:"durationSeconds"`
}
```

<a name="IdleEvent"></a>
## type [IdleEvent](<https://github.com/wu/keyop/blob/main/x/idle/service.go#L33-L39>)

IdleEvent represents a typed payload for idle events.

```go
type IdleEvent struct {
    Now                   time.Time `json:"now"`
    Hostname              string    `json:"hostname"`
    Status                string    `json:"status"`
    IdleDurationSeconds   float64   `json:"idleDurationSeconds"`
    ActiveDurationSeconds float64   `json:"activeDurationSeconds"`
}
```


<a name="Service"></a>
## type [Service](<https://github.com/wu/keyop/blob/main/x/idle/service.go#L45-L59>)

Service monitors the macOS idle APIs to detect user activity and publishes idle/active events.

```go
type Service struct {
    Deps core.Dependencies
    Cfg  core.ServiceConfig

    isIdle           bool
    lastTransition   time.Time
    lastAlertHours   int
    threshold        time.Duration
    hostname         string
    idleMetricName   string
    activeMetricName string

    queueFileTemplate string
    lastReportDay     time.Time
}
```

<a name="Service.maybeSendIdleReport"></a>
### func \(\*Service\) [maybeSendIdleReport](<https://github.com/wu/keyop/blob/main/x/idle/report.go#L18>)

```go
func (svc *Service) maybeSendIdleReport(messenger core.MessengerApi, now time.Time, force bool) error
```

maybeSendIdleReport sends a daily report for the previous day between 00:00 and 01:00 local time. If force is true the
report will be generated regardless of the current hour \(used for reportOnStartup\).

<a name="ServiceState"></a>
## type [ServiceState](<https://github.com/wu/keyop/blob/main/x/idle/service.go#L62-L67>)

ServiceState holds persistent runtime state for the idle service \(for example, last idle timestamp and alerting
state\).

```go
type ServiceState struct {
IsIdle         bool      `json:"is_idle"`
LastTransition time.Time `json:"last_transition"`
LastAlertHours int       `json:"last_alert_hours"`
LastReportDay  time.Time `json:"last_report_day"`
}
```
